text: Cryogenic distillation is expensive and energy-consuming, and this is even more true for the treatment of dilute gases. While MOFs with polarizing functional groups such as fluoride and chlorine are predicted to yield some of the highest Xe/Kr selectivities for a solid adsorbent, very little is actually known experimentally about the Xe-MOF interaction. Here we introduce a new MOF, named SBMOF-2, which shows under ambient conditions a very high selectivity toward Xe. Experimental breakthrough curves show a Xe/Kr selectivity of 10 at 298 K along with a xenon uptake of 27.07 wt %. Single crystal X-ray diffraction reveals a cavity structure comprising interconnecting cages of about 6 Ã† in diameter held together by a phenyl core framework decorated with hydroxyl functional groups that serves as a template of high affinity toward the polarizable Xenon atoms. Breakthrough curves obtained from detailed simulation studies based on a detailed reaction scheme agree remarkably with experimental data and show sharp elution peaks toward Kr followed by a tail toward Xe indicating suitable purity of Xe at the tail end. At par with one of the best reported MOFs including Ni-MOF-74 and cobalt formate based materials, our material, SBMOF-2, thus has the potential to be highly practical to the purification of noble gases.
